source_pr.title: stats: fix pseudo selectivity for primary key
source_pr.body:
When estimating the pseudo selectivity for primary key equal conditions, it should not exceed `1/row count`.
PTAL @coocood @winoros @zz-jason

symptoms: sql: "select c from t where t.c = 1 and t.a > 1 order by t.d limit 1", (plan/physical_plan_test.go)
failure_signature: sql: "select c from t where t.c = 1 and t.a > 1 order by t.d limit 1", (plan/physical_plan_test.go)
root_cause_explanation: The test was flaky because it relied on nondeterministic result ordering (e.g., missing ORDER BY / unsorted assertions). Result ordering is not guaranteed unless explicitly enforced. This PR stabilizes it by: Sort results / enforce deterministic ordering before assertions to avoid nondeterministic flakiness.
fix_pattern: Sort results / enforce deterministic ordering before assertions to avoid nondeterministic flakiness.

evidence: evidence: sql: "select c from t where t.c = 1 and t.a > 1 order by t.d limit 1", (plan/physical_plan_test.go)
